entries.title: Strong Passwords
entries.content: Use 12+ characters mixing cases, numbers, symbols. Unique per site. Password managers generate and store securely.

entries.title: Phishing
entries.content: Fake emails or sites trick users into revealing credentials. Verify sender, avoid suspicious links, enable 2FA.

entries.title: Encryption
entries.content: AES-256 encrypts data at rest; TLS encrypts data in transit (HTTPS). Keys must be kept secret.

entries.title: Two-Factor Authentication
entries.content: Requires password plus second factor (phone code, app, hardware key). Blocks most account takeovers.

entries.title: SQL Injection
entries.content: Attack inserting malicious SQL via input fields. Prevent with parameterized queries and input validation.
